name: Creation of a Privileged Role for Container
notes:
This rule detects the creation of a privileged role. By default, it's defined as a role having access to all resources with all rights, or having create, update, or delete rights on "secrets" specifically.

Note: The rule response adds the role to the Privileged Role reference set. Adjust the AQL query to include any permission considered as Privileged.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Containers
when the event category for the event is one of the following Authentication.Role Creation Success, Authentication.Role Assignment Success, Audit.Update Activity Succeeded

when the event matches ("Role Name Name Actions" like '%*%' AND "Role Name Name Assigned Resources" like '%*%') OR (("Role Name Name Actions" ilike '%create%' OR "Role Name Name Actions" ilike '%update%' OR "Role Name Name Actions" ilike '%delete%' OR "Role Name Name Actions" like '%*%') AND "Role Name Name Assigned Resources" ilike '%secrets%') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Creation of a Privileged Role for Container on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Containers
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Authentication.Role Creation Success, Authentication.Role Assignment Success, Audit.Update Activity Succeeded

AND when the event matches ("Role Name Name Actions" like '%*%' AND "Role Name Name Assigned Resources" like '%*%') OR (("Role Name Name Actions" ilike '%create%' OR "Role Name Name Actions" ilike '%update%' OR "Role Name Name Actions" ilike '%delete%' OR "Role Name Name Actions" like '%*%') AND "Role Name Name Assigned Resources" ilike '%secrets%') AQL filter query
RED_list: Add the "Resource Name" of the event or flow payload to the Reference Set "Privileged Role"
